docs.title: Yifan Liu
docs.text:
Ph.D. Candidate in Condensed Matter Theory University of California, Riverside

Google Scholar | arXiv | CV

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am a Ph.D. candidate in condensed matter theory at the University of California, Riverside.

My research focuses on moiré materials, electron-phonon coupling, twisted bilayer systems, and interaction-driven quantum phases.

docs.title: Research Interests
docs.text:
• Moiré materials
  • Electron-phonon coupling
  • Twisted bilayer graphene and TMDs
  • Topology and correlated phases

docs.title: Publications
docs.text: 1. Yifan Liu, Robert Dawson, Nathaniel Gabor, et al. Phonon-assisted absorption in Transition Metal Dichalcogenide heterostructures

docs.title: Moiré materials
docs.text: Study of twisted bilayer systems, moiré phonons, and emergent low-energy physics.

docs.title: Electron-phonon coupling
docs.text: Symmetry analysis, momentum-resolved coupling, and optical signatures.

docs.title: Topology and interactions
docs.text: Interaction-driven ordering, Berry curvature, and effective field theory descriptions.
